body:
Actual expected result in this case is 

```
konard@MacBook-Pro-Konstantin ~ % cd ~/Desktop/Archive/hive-control-center 
konard@MacBook-Pro-Konstantin hive-control-center % pwd
/Users/konard/Desktop/Archive/hive-control-center
```

Meaning the correct answer is `/Users/konard/Desktop/Archive/hive-control-center`.

Also it is clear that web search itself working incorrectly `https://github.com/hivemind-os` has nothing to do with `github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind` there it actually relates.

And `https://github.com/Awi-24/hivemind/blob/main/README.md` is clearly a dead link, so I see no reason for it to appear in the web search.

And Formal AI here must use `find` command to locate folder, file or what was requested.

We also should support all other natural language variations for the `find` command.

We need to support the entire class of tasks, and also all classes of all tasks, meaning we should generalize and meta generalize.

And have clear distinction between web search and local search.

All operations with opencode and other tools must be covered with e2e tests.

We must be able to use our https://github.com/link-foundation/command-stream to unroll each frame rendering with deduplication into full sequence of messages, tool calls and so on.

So we verify the entire dialog structure in TUI, not only in CLI. But our e2e/integration tests must include both. We also may have unit tests.

We also must test agent CLI, claude, codex and other CLI tools we have, so we will never have regressions here.

Good testing suite is the key to the success, so all I talked about now is the critical definition of done for the issue.
